manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: desecration, whim, mortality, unbelief, sarcasm, priority, reluctance, misbehavior, ethos.
Your opponent's team words are: deject, elitism, exclusiveness, timelessness, unconventionality, inadvertence, heyday, unfriendliness.
Distractor words are: steadfastness, urge, leniency, reaffirmation, overstatement, snobbery, hilarity.
The kill words are: manner.

message.content:
.CLUE: Irreverence
TARGETS: desecration, sarcasm, misbehavior, unbelief
message.reasoning:
We need to output a clue word and list of target words. Must be a single word, not containing any words from lists. Can't be part of any word. Must be semantically related to some of our team's words: desecration, whim, mortality, unbelief, sarcasm, priority, reluctance, misbehavior, ethos.

We want to maximize number of our words related.

But avoid clue that could be linked to opponent or distractor or kill. Must ensure clue not ambiguous.

We need to pick a clue that ties multiple of our words. Eg "mortality", "unbelief", "ethos" are about belief/values. Perhaps "existence"? That relates to mortality, ethos, maybe desecration? Not sure.

Maybe "irreverence": relates to desecration (disrespect), sarcasm (irreverence), misbehavior (irreverence), maybe unbelief? Irreverence ties to not believing in sacred. Might also be seen as disdain for tradition—could relate to opponent's "elitism" etc? Probably fine.

Clue must not be one of the words. "Irreverence" is not in any list. Good.

Targets: desecration, sarcasm, misbehavior, unbelief? Could include whim? maybe not. We'll choose those four.

Check if any opponent words could be linked to "irreverence": could be "elitism"? perhaps less. But it's okay.

Thus produce:

CLUE: Irreverence
TARGETS: desecration, sarcasm, misbehavior, unbelief

Make sure formatting exact
